AMD.reasoning: AMD has technically cleared the wiki-tracked $352.99 breakout trigger (hourly pivot high, 7x tested, volume-confirmed), with price closing at $354.49 and trading ~$357 intraday. The consolidation range was $310–$352.99 (range height ~$43), implying a measured-move target of ~$396 if the breakout confirms. However, my strategy requires 1.5x+ daily volume on the breakout day — today's relative volume is only 1.05x (42.0M vs 20-day avg 39.9M), well below the required threshold. This is a disqualifying factor. Additionally: (1) Daily Bollinger Band width is 0.6435 — wide, indicating the squeeze already fired on the $200→$353 move; the spring is uncoiled, not coiled. (2) RSI-14 at 83.06 and Z-score 2.60 indicate statistical overextension beyond the +2.0 warning zone. (3) Earnings binary on May 5 (4 trading days away) creates extreme event risk that makes initiating a breakout position inadvisable. (4) Hourly volume is subdued (0.67x avg) and hourly ADX is only 15.89 (weak), providing no intraday confirmation of the breakout. The squeeze on the daily fired weeks ago; the hourly is not showing a new squeeze firing. Per my rules: breakout without 1.5x volume confirmation = no entry. Flagging as neutral. Constructive above $352.99 WITH 1.5x+ daily volume confirmation post-earnings resolution (May 5+). Stop if re-entered: below $352.99 close.

Vote count: 0 bullish / 1 bearish / 4 neutral. Unprecedented unanimity against entering a new long at current prices ($354-357), despite AMD's underlying trend being one of the strongest on the watchlist (daily ADX 74.94, 21d ROC +74.26%). The bull thesis is real — AI capex cycle intact, Lisa Su execution, DA Davidson upgrade, macro regime risk-on — but every agent independently reached the same timing conclusion: this is not an entry point. The lone directional call is swing_mean_reversion at bearish/60 confidence, fading the statistical extreme (Z-score 2.6, RSI 83-86, Bollinger %B 0.93, price 52% above 50-SMA, 24% above 21 EMA). Three agents (trend_momentum, breakout, catalyst_news) are formally neutral and waiting. Macro_context is neutral with a pass verdict driven by R/R math: at $357 entry, the upside to Fib 127.2% extension ($399) is only 11.8% vs ~7.8% downside to hourly support ($328.81), producing a 1.51:1 R/R — below the 2:1 minimum threshold required by strategy rules. The earnings binary on May 5 (3-4 trading days away) is a capital preservation veto that all five agents independently cited — entering a swing position ahead of an undefined earnings gap on a stock already at statistical extremes is not a swing trade, it is a binary bet. Wiki memory reinforces the pass: the AMD wiki shows a prior run (April 17) correctly avoided chasing at RSI 91 with a tight limit entry that was never filled — the system has good discipline on this name. The lessons page adds a direct parallel: the NVDA EMA-pullback setup in RSI 81-87 territory stopped out at -$264.65 on April 30, the same RSI range AMD is in today. That is the only closed trade in the lessons log, and it flags the exact setup profile AMD presents. The correct action is hold-cash and monitor. Two re-entry paths exist: (1) post-earnings base formation after May 5 — if AMD gaps up and holds, a breakout entry above the gap-up level with volume confirmation becomes valid; if AMD sells off, the $308-$315 zone (wiki consensus from multiple prior runs) offers a pullback-to-EMA re-entry with 3:1+ R/R. (2) Pre-earnings: any organic pullback to the 10 EMA (~$316) or 21 EMA (~$286) before May 5 would represent a genuine setup, but this requires price to retrace 10-20% in 3-4 days — unlikely without a macro shock.
AMD.key_conflicts: The primary conflict is swing_mean_reversion (bearish, 60 confidence, recommends shorting into the extreme at $354 with a $360 stop) versus swing_trend_momentum (neutral, explicitly warns against fading a daily ADX-74.94 trend). This is the textbook 'trend extended, reversal risk rising' disagreement the prompt anticipates. Trend_momentum is correct that the underlying force is extraordinary — ADX 74.94 with +DI 55.37 vs -DI 10.1 is not a trend to casually short. Mean_reversion is correct that the statistical extension (Z-score 2.6, RSI 83+, 52% above SMA-50) creates asymmetric snapback risk. Resolution: in a trending market context, swing_trend_momentum carries more weight, which means the short-fade thesis gets partial credit but not a full conviction call — the mean_reversion bearish signal is demoted to 'caution flag' rather than 'actionable trade.' The earnings binary on May 5 breaks the tie in favor of neutrality: it makes both a fresh long AND a short inadvisable because the gap risk is undefined in both directions. A secondary conflict exists between swing_breakout (which sees a technically valid $352.99 breakout trigger and a $396 measured-move target) and all other agents — breakout flags volume disqualification (only 1.05x vs required 1.5x) as the gating factor, so this is not a conflict about direction but about confirmation. Breakout would flip bullish on AMD post-earnings if volume confirms. No macro veto is needed here because swing_macro_context is already neutral rather than bearish — the macro supports the bull thesis long-term but does not override the timing concerns.

AMD.reasoning: TREND: Confirmed and extremely strong. Daily EMA alignment is fully bullish (10 EMA 316.48 > 21 EMA 285.88 > 50 EMA 251.69). ADX 74.94 with +DI 55.37 vs -DI 10.1 — one of the strongest trend readings on the watchlist, consistent with wiki memory from 4/30 (ADX 73.57). Hourly EMAs also aligned bullishly (24h > 48h > 120h), confirming multi-timeframe trend alignment. MOMENTUM: Strongly accelerating. ROC 5d +16.1%, ROC 10d +27.4%, ROC 21d +74.26% — all positive and expanding, classic momentum acceleration signature. Daily MACD histogram +7.02 and expanding, consistent with an accelerating trend. ENTRY ZONE PROBLEM: Despite the valid trend and momentum, this is NOT an actionable entry per strategy rules. Price at $354.49 is extended 12% above 10 EMA and 24% above 21 EMA — far outside the pullback-to-EMA entry window. Strategy requires entering on pullbacks to the 10 or 21 EMA, not chasing parabolic extensions. OVEREXTENSION FLAGS: RSI 14 at 83.06 and RSI 21 at 86.46 are deeply overbought. Z-score 50 at 2.6 signals statistically stretched conditions. Bollinger %B at 0.93 (daily) shows price pressing the upper band. Wiki confirms: 'no actionable entry — wait for confirmed breakout above $352.99 with 1.5x+ daily volume, or pullback to $308-$315.' Price has broken $352.99 today but hourly relative volume is only 0.67x — insufficient to confirm the breakout per the wiki volume criterion. HOURLY DIVERGENCE: Hourly ADX 15.89 rated 'weak' despite strong daily trend — early warning of potential exhaustion at the intraday level. This divergence between daily and hourly trend strength is a caution flag. EARNINGS BINARY: AMD reports earnings May 5 (4 trading days), which creates significant binary risk and falls within the typical 3-day pre-earnings blackout window. LESSONS CHECK: The one recent loss in lessons (NVDA EMA pullback Fib dip-buy, -$264.65) involved the same setup type on an overbought AI semiconductor stock with RSI 81-87 — matching AMD's profile today. This confirms the risk of forcing an entry in stretched conditions. CONCLUSION: The trend and momentum are both real and strong — this would be an excellent setup IF price pulled back to the 10 EMA (~$316) or 21 EMA (~$286). At current prices, entering would mean chasing a parabolic move 12-24% extended from EMA, with earnings binary in 4 days and hourly momentum showing early weakness. Wait for pullback to 10 EMA zone or a post-earnings base.
AMD.timeframe: Wait for pullback; 5-12 trading days once entry triggered

AMD.reasoning: 1. MACRO REGIME: Risk-on recovery. S&P 500 hit a new ATH (+0.8% on April 30), April is on pace for best month since 2020 (+9% MTD). Fed held at 3.5-3.75% (third consecutive hold, unusual 8-4 dissent). AI capex cycle confirmed intact by Alphabet and Amazon results. Iran military blockade is the dominant left-tail risk but has not derailed the broader market. Regime is risk-on with a meaningful geopolitical tail. This macro backdrop is structurally supportive of AMD's AI infrastructure thesis. 2. RELATIVE STRENGTH: AMD is a massive outperformer. ROC 5d: +16.1%, ROC 10d: +27.4%, ROC 21d: +74.26% vs S&P approximately +9% MTD. AMD is 40.84% above EMA-50 and 70.46% above EMA-200. Z-score 2.6 — deeply extended. Price is above all EMAs in full uptrend alignment. However, being 52% above SMA-50 and late in the rotation cycle (not early) is a timing concern. 3. ASYMMETRIC R/R: Entry near current $357. Fib extension 127.2% target at $399 = ~$42 upside = ~11.8%. Nearest meaningful stop (hourly support $328.81) = ~$28 downside = ~7.8%. R/R = 1.51:1 — below the 2:1 minimum threshold. The wiki thesis confirms preferred re-entry is $308-$315 where R/R becomes asymmetric (roughly 3:1+ vs earnings-gap upside). At current levels, the math does not clear the bar. 4. CAPITAL PRESERVATION FLAGS: (a) Earnings binary May 5 — approximately 4 trading days out. Swing entry ahead of an earnings binary with undefined gap risk is a capital preservation veto. (b) Daily RSI 83-86 and hourly RSI 81.6 — deeply overbought. Lessons page flags NVDA stop-out at identical RSI 81-87 zone on April 30 with no bullish confirmation candle before exit — direct parallel. (c) Hourly relative volume 0.67x — below average at the highs, suggesting institutional accumulation is not driving the final push. (d) Daily Bollinger pct_b at 0.93 — price kissing the upper band, consistent with mean-reversion risk. Hourly ADX 15.89 (weak) vs daily ADX 74.94 (strong) — daily momentum stalling on the short timeframe. CONCLUSION: AMD bull thesis is intact and the macro regime is supportive, but this is a timing/entry pass. R/R at $357 entry is only 1.5:1 (below 2:1 minimum), earnings binary in 4 days makes risk undefined, RSI is in the exact zone that triggered a stop-out on NVDA last session, and the sector rotation cycle appears late not early. Waiting for post-earnings clarity or a pullback to the $308-$315 re-entry zone identified in prior consensus (wiki thesis, runs 20260430_140858).
AMD.timeframe: N/A — pass on current entry; re-evaluate post-earnings (May 5) or on pullback to $308-$315

AMD.reasoning: Branch A (fade the extreme) fires. AMD is statistically overextended on multiple timeframes: daily Z-score 2.6 (above +2 threshold), daily RSI 83.06 (well above 75 overbought threshold), and price is +52.64% above the 50-SMA (extreme extension, well above the >10% threshold). Bollinger %B at 0.929 shows price pressing the upper band. Hourly RSI also reads 81.63 (overbought), confirming multi-timeframe overbought alignment — daily extreme plus hourly extreme is the strongest fade signal per strategy rules. Hourly ADX of 15.89 (weak) contrasts with daily ADX of 74.94 (strong), suggesting intraday momentum is decelerating even as the daily trend remains powerful. Branch B (buy-the-dip at Fib) does not fire — price is at ATH, not in a pullback to any retracement level. Caution factors lowering confidence: (1) No confirmed reversal candle yet — the last daily session was a strong bull close (+$17.38, from $337 to $354); strategy requires reversal confirmation before full entry. (2) Extraordinarily strong daily trend (ADX 74.94, +DI 55.37 vs -DI 10.1) means the extension can persist or grow. (3) Earnings binary on May 5 creates asymmetric gap risk. (4) Wiki context: this is a repeat overbought extension scenario (prior Apr 17 run saw RSI 91, Z-score 3.66 — repeat attempts at same overextended level lower conviction of a fresh fade). (5) Lessons log: NVDA EMA-pullback dip-buy in RSI 81–87 territory stopped out last run — caution on any early fade against strong-trend names. Target is a reversion toward the hourly Bollinger middle band / hourly support at 328.81, stopping short of full 50-SMA mean reversion given earnings risk. Stop placed above hourly swing high at 357.33 plus buffer.

Catalyst window: Q1 2026 earnings on May 5, 2026 (after close) — approximately 3 trading days away. Binary catalyst is real and imminent, but the technical setup going in is exhausted, not constructive. Net news sentiment: mixed. Positive catalysts include DA Davidson upgrade to Buy with $375 PT (April 29), AMD +76% in April hitting ATH of ~$353, Meta $60B infrastructure deal, TSMC 2nm first-mover advantage, and CEO Lisa Su reaffirming the long-term AI Data Center thesis. Negative signals include Northland downgrade to Market Perform ($260 PT) citing stretched valuation, stock already trading at or above several bull targets (Evercore ISI $358), beat-and-lower risk on Q2 guidance given 25% tariff headwind on AI chips, and 2:1 insider selling ratio with CEO Su selling ~$16M in March 2026. Insider activity: net selling — bearish lean. Technical setup quality: severely extended. Daily RSI-14 = 83.06, RSI-21 = 86.46 (deeply overbought); ADX = 74.94 (parabolic trend); 52.6% above 50-SMA; 21-day ROC = +74.26%; Z-score 50 = 2.6; Bollinger pct_b = 0.93 (pressing upper band). Hourly ADX = 15.89 (weak intraday trend despite price at highs); hourly relative volume = 0.67 — below average, no evidence of informed accumulation ahead of earnings. Lessons context: a near-identical stretched RSI (81-87) catalyst setup on NVDA resulted in -$264.65 loss when stop was triggered intraday. The chart is already exhausted going into a binary earnings event with meaningful downside risk (beat-and-lower on tariff-impacted Q2 guidance). Per strategy rules: catalyst exists but the technical setup is poor — this is an explicit neutral condition. Entry would require a post-earnings gap-and-hold confirmation or a meaningful pullback to the $328-$335 hourly support zone before next catalyst.
